title: when was asbestos started being used

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: when was asbestos started being used

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Asbestos started being used in ancient times, with evidence of its use found in pottery from the Stone Age. However, it was the industrial revolution in the mid-1800s that saw the widespread use of asbestos in industries such as construction, shipbuilding, and automotive manufacturing due to its properties of heat resistance, strength, and durability. Its use peaked in the mid-20th century before it was discovered to be a health hazard, leading to its ban in many countries.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Before the mid 1800s, people used a variety of natural materials for insulation, such as straw, animal hair, cork, and even mud. In Europe, people would weave straw into mats, which they used to insulate their homes. In the US, corn cobs were often used for insulation, particularly in the Midwest. In some cases, people relied on the thickness of walls or roofs to provide insulation. However, these natural materials had limited effectiveness and were often replaced by more advanced materials, such as asbestos, in the 19th century.
